The dose ranges mentioned in the options pertain specifically to different stages of cancer cervix treated via brachytherapy. Early-stage cancers typically require lower doses compared to locally advanced cases due to varying degrees of tumour burden and response characteristics.
\boxed{B} Based on the clinical guidelines and typical dosimetry recommendations for treating early versus locally advanced cervical cancer, B (\boxed{B}) appears correct since higher doses align better with managing larger volumes of disease characteristic of local advancement while still ensuring effective treatment without excessive toxicity.
